title: hieracium vulgatum subsp. pseudorarium
intro: Hieracium vulgatum subsp. pseudorarium is a subspecies of the lesser hawk's-beard plant, a member of the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its yellow flowers and bitter leaves. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to exhibit potential in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Hieracium vulgatum subsp. pseudorarium include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
